---
title: Quick Start
---

# Installation

Install *Calepin* from source using Cargo:

```bash
git clone https://github.com/vincentarelbundock/calepin
cd calepin
make install
```

This builds the release binary, installs it to `~/.cargo/bin/`, and sets up shell completions.

*Calepin* requires R to be installed on your system for `{r}` code chunks, and Python for `{python}` chunks. Both use persistent subprocesses.

Check your installation:

```bash
calepin -v
```


# Basic usage

Render a `.qmd` file to HTML (the default format):

```bash
calepin document.qmd
```

This produces `document.html` in the same directory.


# Output formats

*Calepin* supports four base formats: `html`, `latex`, `typst`, and `markdown`. Use the `-t`/`--target` flag:

```bash
calepin render document.qmd -t latex    # → document.tex
calepin render document.qmd -t typst    # → document.typ
calepin render document.qmd -t markdown # → document.md
```

You can also set the format in the YAML front matter:

```yaml
---
format: latex
---
```

Or let *Calepin* infer it from the output file extension with `-o`:

```bash
calepin render document.qmd -o paper.tex
```

Targets like `pdf` include a compilation step. For example, `calepin render document.qmd -t pdf` renders to LaTeX and then compiles to PDF via [tectonic](https://tectonic-typesetting.github.io/). Custom targets with compilation are configured in `calepin.toml`.


# Live preview

The `preview` subcommand starts a local server and live-reloads whenever you save the file:

```bash
calepin preview document.qmd
```

This serves the rendered HTML at `http://localhost:3456` by default. Use `-p` to change the port:

```bash
calepin preview document.qmd -p 8080
```

Press `Ctrl+C` to stop the preview server.


# Metadata overrides

The `-s`/`--set` flag overrides YAML front matter values from the command line. This is useful for toggling options without editing the file:

```bash
calepin render document.qmd -s toc=true
calepin render document.qmd -s title="Draft" toc=false number-sections=true
```

Multiple key-value pairs can follow a single `-s`:

```bash
calepin render document.qmd -s bibliography=refs.bib fig-width=6
```


# Quiet mode

Suppress progress messages:

```bash
calepin render document.qmd -q
```


# Shell completions

*Calepin* can generate shell completions for tab-completion of commands and options:

```bash
calepin info completions bash > ~/.bash_completion.d/calepin
calepin info completions zsh > ~/.zfunc/_calepin
calepin info completions fish > ~/.config/fish/completions/calepin.fish
```

Restart your shell or source the file to activate completions.


# Putting it all together

```bash
# Render to HTML with table of contents
calepin render paper.qmd -s toc=true

# Render to PDF via the pdf target, overriding the title
calepin render paper.qmd -t pdf -s title="Final Version"

# Live preview while writing
calepin preview paper.qmd
```
